note: Pull quotes rotated on the home page. Verbatim from the corpus translations; citations are text id + verse per the corpus editions.

quotes.text: With a mind that has nothing to lean on, touch the eternal ground of being. Envision your own self as pure space, open in every direction, enclosed by nothing.
Wherever the mind moves — outward, or to the place where outer and inner meet — right there is the state of Śiva. Where could it move that he does not pervade?
The state of Bhairava is everywhere — in ordinary things, in the plain unselected stuff of experience. Nothing exists outside it. This is the way of non-duality.
Even while living, such a one is already free. Even while moving through action, the body moves on its own.

All action built from layers of latent impression, together with the māyā that shrinks the self to an atom — all of it is dissolved by the movements of your awareness, O Goddess, the way sunlight dissolves darkness.

If you wish to behold Śiva without ceasing through the eye of pure consciousness, to find true being within the living lineage, and to come at last into the fullness that leaves nothing out — then, O mind, draw near to her.
You who are by your very nature the cause of poetry even in those born mute — when will my tongue know the taste of the betel leaf that has rested in the lotus mouth of Vāṇī herself?
